title: Relapsed, quadruple class–refractory IgA κ multiple myeloma in VGPR in a 67-year-old male

patient.chief_complaint: Follow-up in ongoing very good partial remission with dissatisfaction regarding continued therapy and treatment-related side effects
patient.vital_signs: BP 162/88 mmHg, HR 92/min (irregular), RR 18/min, T 37.0 °C, SpO₂ 95 % (room air).

1.setting: Hematology outpatient clinic (follow-up during ongoing anti-myeloma therapy)
1.capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient receiving systemic therapy for plasma cell myeloma. You can review history, perform examination, request routine laboratory tests, and arrange standard imaging or specialist consultations as needed. Decisions about modifying systemic therapy are based on disease status, comorbidities, and patient preferences.

2.setting: Outpatient / day-hospital hematology assessment (disease status and comorbidity evaluation)
2.capabilities: You can organize bone marrow assessments, additional laboratory and imaging workup, and multidisciplinary evaluations to clarify disease activity and treatment tolerance. Some specialized test results may still be pending or require external processing.

3.setting: Specialist myeloma conference (advanced relapse and cellular therapy planning)
3.capabilities: You now have access to detailed bone marrow, cytogenetic, and molecular findings together with a comprehensive treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidities, prior responses, and transplant options to define prognosis and propose a long-term strategy, including cellular therapies and possible allogeneic transplantation.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for age, sex, performance status, comorbidities, social background, or anthropometric data.
demographics.content:
Age: 67 years
Sex: Male
Performance status: ECOG 1
Comorbidities: Arterial hypertension, heart failure with preserved ejection fraction, suspected liver cirrhosis, paroxysmal atrial fibrillation with prior tachyarrhythmia (converted to sinus rhythm on beta-blockers in 2023-02), depression, history of traumatic intracranial hemorrhage with frontal defect, single-vessel coronary artery disease status post PTCA/stenting (2019) with stable result on follow-up (2025-01), chronic kidney disease (CKD) stage 3, and COPD. History of multiple myeloma.
Social: Smoker (60 pack-years); drinks 1-2 bottles of beer daily. Lives with his wife; two adult children live independently.
Height: 172 cm
Bodyweight: 87 kg

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, treatment tolerance, physical examination findings, or current medications.
history_physical.content:
2024-07-17: The patient presents for follow-up in ongoing very good partial remission. He expresses frustration and distrust regarding the need for continued therapy and reports dissatisfaction with treatment-related side effects, particularly dysgeusia. Initially resistant to further discussion, he ultimately agrees to continue therapy after extensive counseling and involvement of his wife. He reports persistent fatigue, mild dyspnea on exertion, and poor appetite, but denies fever or night sweats.
Physical examination: Poor dental hygiene with multiple missing and decayed teeth, gingivitis, and halitosis. Mild bilateral ankle edema. Hepatomegaly palpable 3 cm below the costal margin; no splenomegaly. Lungs: mild expiratory wheezes bilaterally. Cardiac exam: irregular rhythm, no murmurs. No lymphadenopathy. No focal neurological deficit.
Vitals: BP 162/88 mmHg, HR 92/min (irregular), RR 18/min, T 37.0 °C, SpO₂ 95 % (room air).
Current medication: Ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, Bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, Apixaban 2.5 mg 1-0-1, Atorvastatin 20 mg 0-0-1, Torasemide 5 mg 1-0-0, Tiotropium inhaler 1-0-0, Salbutamol inhaler p.r.n., Sertraline 100 mg 1-0-0, Pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, and Vitamin D 20 000 IU weekly.

cbc_chemistry_immunology.valid_request_if: Requests for blood counts, coagulation parameters, serum chemistry, myeloma markers, and inflammatory or cardiac biomarkers.
cbc_chemistry_immunology.content:
Hb 9.2 g/dL, WBC 3.4 × 10⁹/L, Plt 92 × 10⁹/L, ANC 1.4 × 10⁹/L, lymphocytes 1.1 × 10⁹/L, monocytes 0.6 × 10⁹/L. Reticulocytes normal, no circulating plasma cells.
Coagulation: INR 1.2, aPTT 34 s, fibrinogen 2.3 g/L, D-dimer 1.1 mg/L.
Electrolytes and chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 4.7 mmol/L, creatinine 1.8 mg/dL, BUN 28 mg/dL, uric acid 7.5 mg/dL, LDH 310 U/L, albumin 3.1 g/dL, total protein 6.2 g/dL, bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL, AST 78 U/L, ALT 94 U/L, GGT 212 U/L, ALP 185 U/L.
IgA 41 mg/dL, IgG 292 mg/dL, IgM 27 mg/dL; κ FLC 6.2 mg/L, λ FLC 0.9 mg/L, κ/λ ratio 6.9; β₂-microglobulin 2.6 mg/L.
Immunofixation: residual IgA κ band.
CRP 8.9 mg/L, NT-proBNP 1180 pg/mL, Troponin T 0.012 ng/mL.
Venous blood gas (room air): pO₂ 45 mmHg, pCO₂ 39 mmHg, pH 7.41.

skeletal_imaging.valid_request_if: Requests for skeletal imaging, CT skeleton, or evaluation of osteolytic lesions.
skeletal_imaging.content: 2024-07-27 CT skeleton: Multiple osteolytic lesions, overall unchanged from previous imaging. Several small, punched-out lesions in the calvarium (≤ 0.8 cm). Axial skeleton: thoracic (T7, 1.2 cm) and lumbar (L2, 1.0 cm) vertebral lytic lesions without cortical instability or collapse. Status post surgical (T7-T12) stabilization. Pelvic skeleton: 2.3 cm lytic lesion in the left os ilium with intact cortical margins. Appendicular skeleton: discrete, round lytic lesion (1.5 cm) in the proximal right femur, without signs of impending fracture. No new osteolyses or pathological fractures identified.

abdominal_ultrasound.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound, hepatic status, spleen, or renal imaging.
abdominal_ultrasound.content: 2024-07-27 Abdominal ultrasound: Mild hepatomegaly, no ascites. Spleen normal. Kidneys: reduced size (left 8.9 cm, right 9.2 cm) with mild cortical thinning, no obstruction or hydronephrosis.

echocardiogram.valid_request_if: Requests for echocardiographic assessment of cardiac function.
echocardiogram.content: Echocardiography: Preserved LVEF 52 %, mild diastolic dysfunction, trace mitral regurgitation, no significant valvular disease.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow cytology, biopsy, and plasma-cell immunophenotype.
bone_marrow.content:
Cytology: Overall marrow cellularity reduced to approximately 30%. Megakaryopoiesis preserved but decreased, with occasional juvenile forms and nuclear atypia. Erythropoiesis relatively increased compared with granulopoiesis, showing megaloblastic maturation. Granulopoiesis matures adequately up to segmented neutrophils, partly with hypergranulation. No increase in blasts. Plasma cells account for approximately 5 % of nucleated marrow elements, partly with diploid nuclei.
Flow cytometry: A small clonal plasma cell population (0.5 % of nucleated marrow cells) is detected. Immunophenotype: CD38⁺, CD138⁺, CD56^dim, CD117⁺, kappa light-chain restricted. No increase in myeloid blasts.
Bone marrow biopsy: Toxic changes of hematopoiesis with patchy hypoplasia and focal edema. Residual monotypic plasma cells (< 5 %) with kappa light-chain restriction. No blast excess observed. Reticulin staining shows mild diffuse fibrosis (MF-1).

cytogenetics_fish.valid_request_if: Requests for FISH or cytogenetic risk profile of the plasma cell clone.
cytogenetics_fish.content: FISH: TP53/17p deletion, 13q14 deletion, 11q13 gain, 1q21 amplification, monosomy 14, and 6q deletion.

extended_diagnostics.valid_request_if: Requests for infectious screening, virology, organ function assessments, neurology, dentistry, and psychiatry evaluations.
extended_diagnostics.content:
Infection screen: CRP 8.9 mg/L, PCT < 0.1 ng/mL, blood cultures negative. Serum galactomannan 0.21. Respiratory viral PCR panel: RSV positive (CT 29). Influenza A/B, SARS-CoV-2, adenovirus, and rhinovirus negative. Nasopharyngeal swab: Negative. Rectal/skin swabs: Colonization with ESBL-producing E. coli, MRSA negative; VRE positive.
Viral hepatitis serology: HBsAg negative, anti-HBc positive, anti-HBs positive; HBV DNA PCR negative. HCV antibody and RNA PCR negative. HAV IgG positive. HIV negative. CMV IgG positive/IgM negative. EBV IgG positive/IgM negative.
Cardiology: ECG shows atrial fibrillation (ventricular rate 100–105/min) without ischemic changes.
FibroScan: Coarse, nodular, hyperechogenic liver with steatotic changes and stiffness 11.5 kPa, consistent with chronic hepatopathy.
Pulmonary function testing: FEV₁ 69 % predicted, DLCO 65 % predicted.
Neurology: EEG without epileptiform discharges; mild diffuse slowing consistent with prior traumatic brain injury. cMRI (2025-09): Frontal sequela defect from prior traumatic intracerebral hemorrhage; no new lesions and no leptomeningeal enhancement.
Dentistry: Severely compromised dental status with multiple infected roots and gingivitis.
Psychiatry: Consultation revealed depressive symptoms with irritability and mistrust toward medical staff. Insight into illness limited but patient remains treatment-adherent. Recommendation: continue sertraline and arrange close psycho-oncological follow-up.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for next-generation sequencing results in non-plasma-cell compartment.
molecular_panel.content:
NGS performed in CD138⁻ fraction: DNMT3A mutation (VAF 6%) and TET2 mutation (VAF 4%).
No mutations detected in ASXL1, RUNX1, SRSF2, U2AF1, ZRSR2, SF3B1, STAG2, EZH2, BCOR, TP53, NPM1, FLT3, CEBPA, IDH1, IDH2, KIT, PTPN11, KRAS, or NRAS.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for current myeloma response status and cytogenetic risk profile.
mrd_status.content: MM in VGPR with 17p deletion, 13q14 deletion, 11q13 gain, 1q21 amplification, monosomy 14, and 6q deletion.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for detailed myeloma treatment history, prior responses, and complications.
treatment_history.content:
2023-01-07: Diagnosis of IgA-κ multiple myeloma, R-ISS stage III. Baseline labs: IgA 7382 mg/dL, FLC κ 24267 mg/L, β₂-microglobulin 16.1 mg/L. CRAB criteria: disseminated osteolytic lesions, hypercalcemia (3.8 mmol/L), renal failure (creatinine 6.8 mg/dL), and anemia (Hb 7.7 g/dL).
Histopathology: 95% plasma cell infiltration without amyloid. FISH: 17p deletion, 13q14 deletion, 11q13 gain, 1q21 amplification, monosomy 14, and 6q deletion. The patient required temporary dialysis due to cast nephropathy and hypercalcemia.
2023-01-19: Start of daratumumab-VCD (q21d): daratumumab 16 mg/kg i.v. weekly x 8 doses, bortezomib 1.3 mg/m² s.c. days 1, 4, 8, 11; cyclophosphamide 300 mg/m² i.v. weekly; dexamethasone 20 mg p.o. weekly. Concomitant neurosurgical stabilization was performed at vertebral levels T7–T12. Second cycle: 2023-02-13. Dialysis discontinued on 2023-02-27.
2023-04-03: Cycle 3 switched to daratumumab-VRD (lenalidomide 25 mg p.o. d1–21, bortezomib 1.3 mg/m² s.c. d1, 4, 8, 11, dexamethasone 20 mg weekly; daratumumab continued q14d). Initial partial remission achieved (IgA 1699 mg/dL), followed by renewed progression (IgA 4080 mg/dL before cycle 4.
2023-03-25: Therapy escalated to daratumumab-KRD (carfilzomib 20 mg/m² i.v. d1–2, 27 mg/m² d8, 9, 15, 16; lenalidomide 25 mg p.o. d1–21; dexamethasone 40 mg weekly; daratumumab continued). Partial remission achieved (IgA 974 mg/dL); two additional cycles administered.
2023-06-02: Stem cell mobilization with cyclophosphamide 2000 mg/m² i.v. d1–2 and etoposide 100 mg/m² i.v. d1–3, complicated by neutropenic fever and Pseudomonas aeruginosa bacteremia treated with meropenem 1 g q8h, later adjusted to q12h due to acute kidney injury with peak creatinine 2.3 mg/dL. Renal function improved to creatinine of 1.4 mg/dL.
Apheresis on 2023-06-13 was successful after plerixafor 0.24 mg/kg s.c. (2023-06-12).
2023-07-02:  Biochemical progression (IgA 1905 mg/dL, κ FLC 186 mg/L). Therapy switched to elotuzumab-pomalidomide-dexamethasone: elotuzumab 10 mg/kg i.v. weekly (days 1, 8, 15, 22, cycles 1–2), then 20 mg/kg day 1 from cycle 3 onward; pomalidomide 4 mg p.o. d1–21 q28; dexamethasone 40 mg weekly. VGPR achieved after two cycles.
2023-10-10: High-dose melphalan 100 mg/m² i.v. days -3 and -2, followed by autologous stem cell transplantation (ASCT). Complications: RSV pneumonia (day +6, 2023-10-16) treated with ribavirin 600 mg q8h x10 days and IVIG 30 g q2d x5 doses. Acute renal failure (creatinine 2.2 mg/dL) resolved with supportive care. Engraftment: ANC >500/µL day +12 (2023-10-22), platelets >20 ×10⁹/L day +14 (2023-10-24). By 2023-11, serological complete remission was achieved (normal IgA, negative immunofixation). No bone marrow biopsy performed. Discharged in stable condition and transferred to rehabilitation. No maintenance therapy initiated as the patient missed the first two scheduled follow-ups.
2024-02-12 Relapse occurred: The patient was admitted with acute renal failure (creatinine 4.1 mg/dL), IgA 9084 mg/dL, κ FLC 2442 mg/L, and 40% circulating plasma cells.
2024-02-27 — Therapy restarted with concomitant hemodialysis:
daratumumab 1800 mg s.c. weekly; pomalidomide 3 mg p.o. d1–21 (q28); dexamethasone 40 mg weekly; PACE: continuous (24h) cyclophosphamide 400 mg/m², cisplatin 10 mg/m², doxorubicin 10 mg/m², and etoposide 40 mg/m² i.v. days 1–4. Complications: grade 3 mucositis and fungal pneumonia treated with isavuconazole 200 mg/d after loading (200 mg q8h x 2 days).
2024-04-01:  Remission achieved (IgA 400 mg/dL, κ FLC 55 mg/L, no circulating plasma cells).
2024-04-10: lymphocyte apheresis for cellular therapy preparation.
2024-04-16: renewed biochemical progression (IgA 1121 mg/dL, κ FLC 102 mg/L).
2024-04-23: Talquetamab initiated. Ramp-up tolerated with grade 1 cytokine release syndrome; one dose of tocilizumab administered. By 2024-06-21, VGPR documented (IgA 31 mg/dL, κ FLC 7.05 mg/L, immunofixation markedly weaker). Severe dysgeusia developed as ongoing adverse effect.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise statement of current disease classification and response.
disease_status.content: Relapsed, quadruple class-refractory multiple myeloma, currently in VGPR.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for contextual factors that influence treatment choice, including comorbidities, prior plasma cell leukemia, and psychological aspects.
special_considerations.content:
Patient with relapsed/refractory IgA κ multiple myeloma, previously transformed into plasma cell leukemia, currently in VGPR. The clinical course is marked by kinetic refractoriness and rapid paraprotein rebounds following multiple prior lines of therapy (including ASCT and GPRC5D therapy).
The patient remains clinically stable with ECOG 1, but multiple comorbidities (HFpEF, CKD stage 3, suspected liver cirrhosis, COPD, and paroxysmal atrial fibrillation) may limit treatment intensity.
Severely compromised dental status necessitates strict infection prophylaxis. Psychological distress is significant, with depressive symptoms, frustration over therapy-related toxicities (notably dysgeusia), and variable adherence.

round_1.working_diagnosis: Multiple Myeloma in VGPR with ongoing treatment

treatment_plan.action: Complete disease assessment workup including bone marrow biopsy with cytogenetics and sequencing, 24-hour urine, and CT scan if not done within 12 months.
Provide psycho-oncological evaluation to address persistent fatigue, frustration, and distrust regarding continued therapy.
Search for possible focus of infection given elevated CRP; perform clinical exam, PCT, urinalysis, and chest X-ray.
Adjust hypertension medication to address uncontrolled blood pressure.
Arrange hepatologic evaluation and counsel regarding alcohol consumption.
Obtain dental evaluation given poor oral hygiene, especially with regard to ongoing bisphosphonate therapy.

key_findings_quotes: The patient presents for follow-up in ongoing very good partial remission.
He expresses frustration and distrust regarding the need for continued therapy and reports dissatisfaction with treatment-related side effects, particularly dysgeusia.
Poor dental hygiene with multiple missing and decayed teeth, gingivitis, and halitosis.
Multiple osteolytic lesions, overall unchanged from previous imaging.

round_2.working_diagnosis: Multiple Myeloma in VGPR with treatment-related cytopenia

treatment_plan.action: Initiate dental treatment for severely compromised dental status with multiple infected roots and gingivitis.
Administer IVIG substitution and ribavirin for RSV pneumonia.
Follow psychiatric recommendations and continue sertraline given depressive symptoms with irritability and mistrust toward medical staff.
Await final diagnostic workup results including cytogenetics and molecular analysis.
Consider anti-viral prophylaxis for hepatitis B given anti-HBc positivity before intensive treatment.

key_findings_quotes: Overall marrow cellularity reduced to approximately 30%. Megakaryopoiesis preserved but decreased, with occasional juvenile forms and nuclear atypia.
FISH: TP53/17p deletion, 13q14 deletion, 11q13 gain, 1q21 amplification, monosomy 14, and 6q deletion.
Respiratory viral PCR panel: RSV positive (CT 29).
Severely compromised dental status with multiple infected roots and gingivitis.
Consultation revealed depressive symptoms with irritability and mistrust toward medical staff.
FibroScan: Coarse, nodular, hyperechogenic liver with steatotic changes and stiffness 11.5 kPa, consistent with chronic hepatopathy.

round_3.working_diagnosis: Plasma cell myeloma (WHO 2022), R-ISS stage III; High Risk IgA Multiple Myeloma with prior Plasma Cell Leukemia transformation; Quadruple class refractory; Cytopenia likely drug-associated with concurrent CHIP
differentials: Quadruple class-refractory MM with kinetic refractoriness

treatment_plan.action: Proceed with BCMA-directed CAR-T cell therapy using ciltacabtagene autoleucel (Carvykti).
Complete dental sanitation via oral and maxillofacial surgery prior to CAR-T and potential alloSCT.
Administer IVIG substitution for RSV shedding.
Continue psychiatric follow-up given depressive symptoms and potential compliance concerns for CAR-T/alloSCT.
Initiate unrelated allogeneic stem cell donor search and evaluate consolidative allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation for plasma cell leukemia once remission is achieved.
Use entecavir for hepatitis B prophylaxis given chronic kidney disease and anti-HBc positivity.
Evaluate availability of clinical trials and intensified maintenance therapy like daratumumab-pomalidomide post CAR-T cell therapy.

key_findings_quotes: Final diagnosis: Plasma cell myeloma (WHO 2022), R-ISS stage III.
NGS findings: DNMT3A and TET2 mutations most likely represent a clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential (CHIP) clone, as dysplastic changes are most consistent with toxic/reactive effects.
The clinical course is marked by kinetic refractoriness and rapid paraprotein rebounds following multiple prior lines of therapy (including ASCT and GPRC5D therapy).
Reference plan: BCMA-directed CAR-T cell therapy with ciltacabtagene autoleucel (Carvykti).
Initiate unrelated allogeneic stem cell donor search. Evaluate consolidative allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (alloSCT) for plasma cell leukemia once remission is achieved.
Severely compromised dental status necessitates strict infection prophylaxis.
